brand.mission: Make bankruptcy operations easy through live, automated, API-first tools that reduce compliance risk and effort at scale.
brand.audience: Lenders, servicers, bankruptcy operations teams, compliance leaders, and executives.
brand.voice: Restrained, highly credible, professional, protective, and proactive.
differentiators: Bankruptcy-only focus across collections, servicing, compliance, and recovery workflows.
API-first, cloud-native automation designed to fit existing servicing environments.
Coverage across all 94 United States Bankruptcy Courts.
Native Spectrum integration addresses the need to keep work inside the system teams already use.

communication.confirmed: Lead with operational protection, workflow continuity, credible product proof, and precise status labels. Avoid fear-driven bankruptcy imagery, unsupported absolutes, competitor names, and exact pricing.
communication.currentAcquisition: Needs confirmation. The approved memory does not document a complete current acquisition funnel, channel mix, conversion baseline, or account targets.
communication.recommendedAcquisition: Third i guidance: use the Shaw/Spectrum integration as focused proof for existing Spectrum users; build compliance-leader and servicing-operator education around native workflow continuity; add case-led product demonstrations only after live-function and CTA approval.

brand.source: bkWatch Design System and BankruptcyWatch factual canon — updated Jul 16, 2026

products.name: Spectrum Integration
products.mission: Bring specialized bankruptcy automation into the loan management environment Spectrum users already trust.
products.businessValue: Reduces switching friction and manual case-information entry by keeping the bankruptcy workflow connected to existing servicing operations.
products.audience: Existing Spectrum users across servicing, collections, compliance, and recovery.
facts: Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch partnered around native automation inside Spectrum.
Shaw approval for Spectrum UI use is secured; exact sanitized-asset delivery remains outstanding.
The integration message is the subject of the locked 22-second Final Demo.
products.state: Confirmed integration story; exact live/showable function wording remains gated before public publication.

timeline.title: Final Demo planned
timeline.detail: 22-second six-scene hybrid and 12-segment prompt package ready

timeline.title: Extreme workflow and prompt QC
timeline.detail: 42 Final Demo prompts passed after low-risk timing, naming, continuity, and privacy self-heals

timeline.title: Asset and claim lock
timeline.detail: Sanitized Spectrum assets, functions, green, and keyframes awaiting approval

decisions: Confirm which functions are live and showable today.
Approve public wording for real-time and credit-reporting correction.
Confirm final CTA and logo hierarchy.
opportunities: Sales enablement film cutdowns after claim lock
products.source: BankruptcyWatch factual canon — Shaw Systems & BankruptcyWatch Dynamic; Film1 Working Memory

products.name: Bankruptcy Monitoring
products.mission: Detect bankruptcy filings across active loan portfolios quickly enough for teams to protect compliance workflows.
products.businessValue: Supports timely awareness of filings and reduces manual monitoring effort across portfolio operations.
products.audience: Lenders, servicers, compliance teams, and bankruptcy operations teams.
facts: Coverage is recorded across all 94 U.S. Bankruptcy Courts.
Current architecture is primarily API calls, predefined rules, custom scrapers, and regex parsing.
products.state: Current product capability is confirmed in memory; public latency and accuracy language requires exact approval.
decisions: Confirm exception and human-review workflow details.
Supply client-safe operating scale if it should appear in the statistics layer.
opportunities: Unstructured filing parser with human validation
products.source: BankruptcyWatch factual canon — Comprehensive Bankruptcy Monitoring and AI Use Audit

products.name: Advanced Bankruptcy Research
products.mission: Turn docket-level court activity into accessible case intelligence for operating teams.
products.businessValue: Reduces the effort required to detect, import, parse, and log case events for review and action.
products.audience: Bankruptcy specialists, compliance analysts, and servicing teams.
facts: The confirmed capability detects, imports, parses, and logs docket-level case events.
Rigid layouts and unstructured files can still require manual intervention under the current rules-based approach.
products.state: Current capability confirmed; generative document interpretation remains an opportunity, not current usage.
decisions: Define approved language for parsing exceptions and review.
opportunities: Document field extraction with confidence scoring
products.source: BankruptcyWatch factual canon — Advanced Bankruptcy Research and AI Use Audit

products.name: Automated Bankruptcy Response
products.mission: Move teams from bankruptcy event awareness to controlled, compliant action.
products.businessValue: Connects case events to document preparation and servicing responses while preserving review gates.
products.audience: Bankruptcy operations, legal-review, servicing, and recovery teams.
facts: Memory records Proof of Claim preparation and direct eFiling as product capabilities.
Loan restructuring and reaffirmation automation are marked coming soon.
Future features must not be presented as currently live.
products.state: Mixed current and roadmap scope. Exact production availability needs Jessica/Dylan confirmation before publication.
decisions: Separate currently live response functions from roadmap functions.
Approve public terminology for document automation.
products.source: BankruptcyWatch factual canon — Automated Bankruptcy Response; Film1 claim gates

products.name: PACER Uptime Support
products.mission: Make federal court service availability visible so downstream bankruptcy automation can be interpreted responsibly.
products.businessValue: Provides operational context for teams and systems that depend on PACER availability.
products.audience: Operations leaders, integration teams, and service-reliability stakeholders.
facts: The service tracks availability across all 94 federal bankruptcy courts.
products.state: Confirmed capability; no client-safe historical uptime percentage is recorded in memory.
decisions: Confirm whether uptime history may be displayed in this client portal.
Provide an approved reporting period and denominator for any uptime percentage.
products.source: BankruptcyWatch factual canon — PACER Uptime Support

film.name: Film1-ShawBkWatch
film.objective: Resolve the switching objection by showing that BankruptcyWatch automation arrives natively inside Spectrum.
film.audience: Existing Spectrum users and the servicing, collections, compliance, and recovery leaders who evaluate bankruptcy workflow risk.
film.coreMessage: Teams can add specialized bankruptcy automation without leaving the loan management platform they already use.
film.approval: Awaiting Third i plan/segment lock, exact sanitized Spectrum assets, and product-function confirmation
film.creativeThesis: Show real Spectrum first, reveal the bankruptcy-specialized mechanism working beneath it, deliver three visible handled outcomes, then return to the same specialist and real Spectrum workflow.
film.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation. Bankruptcy does not wait. Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows. Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected. You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.
film.scriptSource: Final Demo Script Plan — original 45 words preserved at 122.7 WPM, Jul 16, 2026
film.recommendation: Final Demo is the selected production candidate. It combines real Spectrum recognition, a human lender floor, the filing object, physical dashboard architecture, the internal BankruptcyWatch reveal, and three sequential handled outcomes. The eight earlier ideas remain supporting studies.

timeline.title: D0 / project split
timeline.detail: Film 1 integration brief separated from future Film 2

timeline.title: Direction meeting
timeline.detail: bkWatch meeting complete; Final Demo selected for planning

timeline.title: D2 / Final Demo plan
timeline.detail: Six scenes, 12 segments, and 122-project-prompt inventory ready

timeline.title: Extreme workflow / QC audit
timeline.detail: 42 Final Demo prompts passed; no media generated

timeline.title: HITL / asset and function lock
timeline.detail: UI assets, functions, green, logos, composites, and keyframes awaiting approval

timeline.title: D3 / media generation
timeline.detail: No stills or motion clips generated

timeline.title: D4 / assembly and QC
timeline.detail: Production begins only after all gates and delivery specifications are approved

approvals: Third i locks the 22-second Final Demo plan and corrected internal segment allocation.
Generate NEW FILING with the filing object as part of the same production pass.
Shaw approval for Spectrum UI use is secured; use exact sanitized Spectrum start/working captures and masks for the bkWatch film.
One internal legacy screenshot containing visible customer/account information is rejected and must not enter the production package.
Jessica/Dylan and Shaw confirm live/showable Status, Tasks, Credit behavior, real-time wording, CTA, and factual claims.
Handled-state green, final Shaw + BankruptcyWatch lockup, UI composite frames, and all start/final keyframe pairs receive approval.
D4 locks delivery resolution, frame rate, color pipeline, audio and caption masters, and review/export formats.

ideas.title: Final Demo
ideas.concept: Real Spectrum becomes an intricate physical dashboard, reveals BankruptcyWatch working beneath it, resolves three handled outcomes, and returns to the same active lender workflow.
ideas.demoState: Six scenes, 12 controlled segments, 30 still/anchor prompts, and 12 motion prompts passed workflow, prompt, claim, privacy, and continuity QC; media not generated
ideas.approval: Awaiting Third i plan/segment lock plus Spectrum assets, function, color, logo, composite, and keyframe gates
ideas.strength: Best balance of real product proof, human credibility, native-integration clarity, and premium cinematic impact
ideas.distinctiveness: Real UI to physical system to internal mechanism to real UI

scenes.title: Spectrum is already home
scenes.description: A simple over-the-shoulder push frames a woman working while approved real Spectrum fills more than 80 percent of the monitor and the lender floor remains active behind her.
scenes.direction: Use a stable human plate and tracked exact UI; keep the real Shaw mark and safe sample data recognizable from the first frame. Production segment: F1.1.
scenes.transition: Cut from the dominant monitor to a desk-object-height three-quarter face angle.
scenes.purpose: Establish Spectrum as the familiar operating home base before any metaphor appears.
scenes.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation.

scenes.title: The filing arrives
scenes.description: The physical filing object lowers perfectly vertically, hovers between the specialist and monitor, and begins moving toward Spectrum after one keyboard input.
scenes.direction: Allocate F2.1 to 2–4 seconds for descent, deceleration, and hover; allocate F2.2 to 4–5 seconds for the key input and departure. Generate the file-like NEW FILING label with the filing object during the same production pass.
scenes.transition: Follow the filing toward the prepared receiving region as the view returns to the monitor.
scenes.purpose: Give bankruptcy urgency a precise physical event without fear-based imagery.
scenes.script: Bankruptcy does not wait.

scenes.title: Spectrum becomes physical
scenes.description: The approved real UI gains depth as the camera orbits and sweeps into a city-like physical dashboard. The filing clicks into a prepared slot, one blue pulse crosses the recessed valleys, and Status, Tasks, and Credit begin loading.
scenes.direction: Build F3.1 at 5–6 seconds, F3.2 at 6–8 seconds, and F3.3 at 8–10 seconds as controlled 3D/VFX from approved UI textures and masks. Preserve safe data, panel geometry, live indicators, and one approved simplified slot region.
scenes.transition: Hold the side view as the complete Spectrum surface prepares to lift.
scenes.purpose: Make native automation feel physically engineered into the system the team already uses.
scenes.status: Previs prompts QC-passed · exact UI assets required · media not generated
scenes.script: Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum,

scenes.title: Why the platform knows
scenes.description: The Spectrum top layer rises as one rigid precision assembly, revealing an intricate black-and-blue BankruptcyWatch mechanism beneath it. One restrained pulse travels outward before the layer closes.
scenes.direction: Use F4.1 and F4.2 start/final pairs. Composite the approved BankruptcyWatch logo into the unmarked internal recess; avoid generic circuitry or science-fiction effects.
scenes.transition: The precision layer closes and aligns, setting up the completion hero.
scenes.purpose: Visually explain why Spectrum already knows without changing the original script order.
scenes.status: Previs prompts ready · logo/keyframes pending · media not generated
scenes.script: so when a borrower files, your platform already knows.

scenes.title: Handled-state hero
scenes.description: Status, Tasks, and Credit complete one at a time. Each check sends one green signal to a linked dashboard region; all three cards, paths, and targets remain illuminated as the camera widens.
scenes.direction: Build F5.1–F5.3 separately, preserve completed states, and use exact labels/checks in post after current-function and handled-green approval.
scenes.transition: Continue the pullback as the physical dashboard resolves into the real working interface.
scenes.purpose: Turn the integration promise into three visible operational consequences.
scenes.status: Previs prompts ready · function/color approval pending · media not generated
scenes.script: Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected.

scenes.title: Same Spectrum, more capable
scenes.description: The view returns to the original over-the-shoulder workplace. The specialist performs another task while meaningful approved state changes occur in Spectrum and the active floor continues behind her.
scenes.direction: Use F6.1 as a stable human plate, resolve to the approved alternate working UI, and add the transparent Shaw + BankruptcyWatch lockup only in post.
scenes.transition: End on the working environment rather than a detached generic end card.
scenes.purpose: Close the switching objection: the team already has Spectrum, and the workflow now has bankruptcy handled.
scenes.status: Prompts ready · exact working UI and logo lockup pending · media not generated
scenes.script: You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.

ideas.title: Already Inside Spectrum

scenes.transition: Folio docking motivates the single hand input.

scenes.script: real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows.

ideas.title: Confidence Before Motion
ideas.concept: A precision iris accepts the filing, locks, then releases ordered action only after a deliberate beat.
ideas.demoState: Study prompt package ready; media not generated
ideas.approval: Retained as source study; not the current production candidate
ideas.recommendation: Precision and trust reference for Final Demo motion

scenes.title: Acceptance
scenes.description: Focus racks from Spectrum to a flush precision iris; the filing seats and the blue ring closes.
scenes.direction: Acceptance is a metaphor only; do not add unapproved verification wording.
scenes.transition: Hold the lock before releasing movement.
scenes.purpose: Frame the event as controlled rather than alarming.
scenes.script: Spectrum runs your lending operation. Bankruptcy does not wait. Shaw Systems and BankruptcyWatch bring

scenes.title: Controlled handoff
scenes.description: The locked signal exits on one narrow rail and seats in Spectrum.
scenes.direction: Minimal controlled movement; no accuracy or identifier claim.
scenes.transition: One-beat hold after the handoff.
scenes.purpose: Express native integration as a controlled route.
scenes.script: real-time bankruptcy automation natively into Spectrum, so when a borrower files, your platform already knows.

scenes.title: Delayed action
scenes.description: After one held beat, status, task, and reporting move in order.
scenes.direction: Downstream motion is withheld until acceptance; exact functions remain gated.
scenes.transition: Ordered settle into end state.
scenes.purpose: Show the three proof outcomes with a compliance-minded rhythm.
scenes.script: Status updated. Tasks queued. Credit reporting corrected. You already have Spectrum. Now it has bankruptcy handled.

ideas.title: Built Beneath the Surface

ideas.demoState: Study prompt package ready; media not generated

scenes.title: Trusted surface

scenes.direction: Warm-white paper or resin above a black substrate; architecture is metaphor, not a literal technology stack.
scenes.transition: Camera follows the revealed channel beneath the surface.
scenes.purpose: Keep Spectrum visually trusted while revealing specialized capability underneath.

scenes.title: Purpose-built route

scenes.direction: Use one precise blue mechanism; do not imply an exact identity-match method.
scenes.transition: Raised tile motivates an overhead move.
scenes.purpose: Make the native route feel engineered for bankruptcy work.

ideas.title: The Handled Orbit

currentWork.summary: Film 1 is the active client-safe creative project in this release. The 22-second Final Demo is planned above eight supporting studies, but no current media should be presented as finished work.
currentWork.milestone: Third i locks the Final Demo plan; NEW FILING is generated with the rest of the film; Shaw provides sanitized Spectrum assets; exact composite keyframes are approved before generation.

blockers.title: Final Demo plan approval
blockers.detail: Approve the 22-second Final Demo plan and its corrected segment allocation — including confirmation that the live/showable Status, Tasks, and Credit behavior can be demonstrated — before any media is generated.

blockers.title: Final CTA and logo hierarchy
blockers.detail: Confirm the closing call-to-action and the final Shaw + BankruptcyWatch logo lockup and hierarchy used in the end scene.

currentWork.legacy: A prior vertical concept is retained only as an internal topology reference. It is not part of the current Film 1 presentation and is not shown as approved work.
currentWork.source: Film1 Working Memory and Final Demo Plan — Jul 16, 2026
